2605.27022.title: ORCA: An End-to-End Interactive Copilot for Optimized Root Cause Analysis
2605.27022.paper_summary: Causal analysis is a crucial task in many domains, including manufacturing, social science, and medicine. However, despite recent progress, the conceptual and methodological complexity of causal methods makes them largely inaccessible to domain experts. This gap prevents experts from leveraging these advances and hinders researchers who lack access to real-world data for validation. To bridge this divide, we introduce ORCA, a copilot for end-to-end causal analysis. ORCA orchestrates agents to understand the user's goals and guide them through the most appropriate causal analysis workflow, from fully automatic to highly user-guided execution. It features causal discovery, causal effect estimation, explainability and Root-Cause-Analysis (RCA). ORCA evaluates and compares performance, generates key metrics and diagrams, and generates insights through structured reports. We highlight its effectiveness across several real-world use-cases.
2605.27022.paper_summary_zh: 因果分析在許多領域中都是一項關鍵任務，包括製造業、社會科學和醫學。<lb/>然而，儘管最近取得了一些進展，因果方法的概念和方法論複雜性使其對領域專家來說大多不可及。<lb/>這一差距阻礙了專家利用這些進展，並妨礙了缺乏真實數據進行驗證的研究人員。<lb/>為了彌合這一鴻溝，我們介紹了 ORCA，一個端到端因果分析的副駕駛。<lb/>ORCA 協調代理以理解用戶的目標，並引導他們通過最合適的因果分析工作流程，從完全自動到高度用戶引導的執行。<lb/>它具有因果發現、因果效應估計、可解釋性和根本原因分析 (RCA)。<lb/>ORCA 評估和比較性能，生成關鍵指標和圖表，並通過結構化報告產生洞見。<lb/>我們強調它在幾個真實案例中的有效性。
2605.27022.author: Phi Nguyen Xuan et.al.
2605.27022.authors: Phi Nguyen Xuan, Nicholas Tagliapietra, Lavdim Halilaj, Kristian Kersting, Juergen Luettin
